summary: Frontend-only validation completed for iOS Safari Azan/recitation fixes and version 3.2.1 changelog flow.

integration_issues.flow: Recitation iPhone Safari support banner copy rendering
integration_issues.issue: In Chromium-based automation, iPhone Safari banner text could not be reliably forced to render even with UA/webkitSpeechRecognition stubs; code path exists but requires physical iPhone Safari validation.

action_items: Run physical iPhone Safari verification for recitation iOS support banner/help copy and microphone permission UX.
Run physical iPhone Safari verification for Azan playback strategy (HTTPS-first with local fallback) under real autoplay/device audio policies.
critical_code_review_comments: SettingsPage.tsx remains very large (~2200+ lines), increasing regression risk and making iOS-specific behavior harder to isolate and test.
Recitation iOS support depends on runtime browser detection; maintainability would improve with explicit test hooks/data-testid coverage for support banners.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Confirmed via UI automation: Settings Azan test button exists and is clickable, hidden fallback audio element exists with inline playback attributes, changelog popup flow can show 3.2.1 for older localStorage version, no blocking runtime errors observed (only non-blocking warnings). iPhone-specific recitation support text exists in source code and unsupported hard-code removed, but physical iPhone Safari is still required for final behavioral verification.
rca of the issue: Primary remaining gap is environment limitation, not clear regression: Chromium automation cannot fully replicate iPhone Safari media/mic policy and browser detection behavior. Reproduction/mitigation: validate on real iPhone Safari (iOS 16+ and one <14.5 case), confirm speech support banner state, direct-tap mic start, and HTTPS-first Azan playback fallback order under real user gesture constraints.
